body.title: Rack devices re-register after web server restart
body.result: flat-file persistence for agent registrations — _save_agents/_load_agents in server.py, restored on startup; 5 tests
body.description:
When the web server restarts, all agent registrations are lost from _agents dict. Devices registered at boot (Granny, Librarian) don't re-register, so their tabs disappear from the web UI.

Proposed shape: on WebSocket connect, server broadcasts a 'server_restarted' or 'announce_yourselves' event. Devices listening (via their existing WS or HTTP poll loop) call /api/agents/register again. Alternatively, web server persists registrations to a flat file on disk and reloads on startup.

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py, devices/granny/daemon.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; flat-file or Postgres only for persistence
**Scope boundary:** registration persistence across server restarts; not full device lifecycle management
**Completion criteria:** restart web server → Granny's tab reappears in UI within one poll cycle without manual intervention
